title: silene cucubalus proles maritima
intro: Silene cucubalus proles maritima, also known as coastal catchfly, is a subspecies of the Silene cucubalus plant that is native to coastal areas and is characterized by its bright pink to red flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Silene cucubalus proles maritima include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the flowers and leaves, which can be dried, powdered, and used in teas, infusions, or topical applications.
